provider.title: Provider
provider.description: Configure provider connections separately for Claude and Codex engines. Credentials are encrypted and stored locally.
provider.defaultEngineHint: Used by default for new sessions. You can still override it per session.

modes.subscriptionDesc: Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise — login via browser OAuth
modes.apiKeyDesc: Anthropic Console — pay per API usage
modes.openrouterDesc: Use Claude via OpenRouter — third-party Anthropic API proxy
modes.customDesc: Any Claude-compatible endpoint — self-hosted proxy or third-party gateway

modesCodex.openrouterDesc: Use OpenRouter as an OpenAI-compatible backend for Codex
modesCodex.customDesc: Configure your own OpenAI-compatible gateway (Codex uses Bearer auth)

provider.codexHint: Codex currently supports OpenAI-compatible providers (OpenRouter / Custom API).
provider.unsupportedModeForEngine: Mode {{mode}} is not supported for {{engine}}. Please choose a supported mode.
provider.customAuthStyleCodexFixed: Codex engine always uses Bearer Token (Authorization: Bearer).
provider.customAuthStyleHint_api_key: Key is sent as the x-api-key header (standard Anthropic format).
provider.customAuthStyleHint_bearer: Key is sent as Authorization: Bearer (OpenRouter-style).
provider.defaultModelPlaceholder: Select or type a model ID
provider.defaultModelHint: Override the default model for all new sessions. Leave blank to use the provider's default.
provider.defaultReasoningEffortHint: Applied as Codex model_reasoning_effort for new sessions. Defaults to high; switch when needed.
provider.loginHelp: Opens your browser to sign in with your Claude Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise account.

overview.title: Engine Overview
overview.description: Check both engine states first, then configure the active engine.

provider.selectedEngineDescription: Complete setup for the selected engine step by step.

modeCredential.title: Choose Mode and Configure Credentials
modeCredential.description: Select a provider mode, then enter credentials for that mode.

mode.title: Choose Provider Mode
mode.description: Select how this engine should connect.

credential.title: Configure Credentials
credential.description: Enter and save credentials required by the selected mode.
credential.empty: Choose a mode in Step 1 first.

model.title: Set Default Model (Optional)
model.description: Type a model ID or choose from built-in suggestions.
model.empty: Select a mode before setting a default model.

network.title: Proxy Configuration
network.description: Global proxy for Claude Code sessions and messaging connections.
network.errorMissingScheme: Must include a scheme, e.g. http:// or socks5://

command.title: Chat
command.description: Default parameters for new AI sessions (Max Turns currently applies to Claude sessions only).

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Control when OpenCow shows desktop notifications.
notifications.onErrorDesc: Notify when a session encounters an error
notifications.onStatusChangeDesc: Notify when a session needs attention

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Send event notifications to external services like Lark, Telegram, or custom webhooks.
webhooks.noWebhooksDesc: Add a webhook to receive event notifications in Lark, Telegram, or any HTTP endpoint.

edit.selectOneEvent: Please select at least one event.
edit.useProxyDesc: Route requests through the global proxy configured in Settings → Network (useful for services like Telegram that require a proxy).

providerDesc.custom: Generic JSON POST to any URL

lark.secretPlaceholder: Leave empty if signing is not enabled

messaging.title: IM Bots
messaging.description: Connect Telegram, Feishu, Discord, WeChat — chat with AI from your messaging apps.

emptyState.title: Connect your first IM bot
emptyState.description: Choose a platform to get started. Setup takes just a few minutes.

messaging.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this connection? This action cannot be undone.
messaging.adapterInDev: Backend adapter is under development. You can pre-configure credentials now — they will be saved and ready when the adapter ships.
messaging.allowedUserIdsPlaceholder: Comma-separated IDs (leave blank to allow everyone)
messaging.workspaceDesc: Sessions from this connection use this working directory.

telegram.setupDesc: Follow these steps to connect your Telegram Bot

steps.createBotDesc: Open Telegram, search for @BotFather, and send /newbot to create a new bot.
steps.copyTokenDesc: BotFather will give you an API token. Paste it into the Bot Token field.
steps.getChatIdDesc: Send a message to your bot, then visit api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/getUpdates to find your chat ID.
steps.testAndStartDesc: Click Test Connection to verify, then enable the bot to start receiving messages.

telegram.helpCommand: Once connected, use /help in Telegram to see all available commands.

weixin.scanToLogin: Scan QR code with WeChat to connect
weixin.singleDeviceWarning: Only one bot session per WeChat account
weixin.grayTestNote: WeChat ClawBot is in gray-test phase. Ensure your account has access.

botCreator.title: AI Bot Setup
botCreator.welcomeTitle: Set up a new IM bot
botCreator.welcomeDescription: Tell me which platform you'd like to use, and I'll guide you through the setup step by step.
botCreator.inputPlaceholder: Describe the bot you want to set up...
botCreator.discardMessage: Your AI-generated bot configuration hasn't been created yet. If you close now, it will be lost.
botCreator.aiSetupTooltip: Use AI to guide you through bot setup step by step

guide.telegram: {{name}} has been saved. Go to the connection card to enable it, then send /start in Telegram to begin chatting.
guide.feishu: {{name}} has been saved. Go to the connection card to enable it, then send a message in your Feishu bot chat to begin.
guide.discord: {{name}} has been saved. Go to the connection card to enable it, then mention the bot in a Discord channel to begin.
guide.weixin: {{name}} has been saved. Go to the connection card to scan QR code and enable it, then send a message in WeChat to begin.

evose.title: EvoseAI Integration
evose.description: Evose is where humans and AI agents work together as one team. Build custom AI agents for your needs. Chat, assign, collaborate—just like messaging a teammate.
evose.mcpDescription: Register EvoseAI Agents and Workflows as MCP tools for OpenCow Agent to call directly.
evose.connectionFailed: Connection failed, please check your configuration
evose.endpointHint: For private deployments. Leave blank to use the default endpoint

updates.title: Updates
updates.description: Configure how OpenCow checks for new versions via GitHub Releases.
updates.autoCheckDesc: Periodically check GitHub Releases for new versions.
